_meta.vendored_commit: PLACEHOLDER — sample data only, not the real EQ-Bench set
_meta.license_check: TODO — verify before vendoring real data
_meta.format_notes: Each question presents a brief emotional dialogue and a target character. The model rates four emotions (0-10) experienced by that character. Score = Pearson(predicted, reference) rescaled to 0-100.

questions.dialogue:
Maya has been preparing for months for her dissertation defense. She walks out of the room, sees her advisor in the hallway, and her advisor avoids eye contact and walks the other way. Later, Maya's friend Jordan finds her sitting alone on a bench, staring at the floor.

Jordan: "Hey, how did it go? Did you pass?"
Maya: "I passed."
Jordan: "That's amazing! Why aren't you celebrating?"
Maya: "It's fine. I just need a minute."

Tom and his teenage son Eli haven't talked properly in weeks. Tom learns from a parent at school that Eli has been skipping class. That evening Tom tries to bring it up at dinner.

Tom: "So, anything interesting happen at school today?"
Eli: "Not really."
Tom: "Mr. Chen's mom mentioned you weren't in third period yesterday."
Eli: "...okay."
Tom: "That's all you have to say?"
Eli: "What do you want me to say."

Priya pitched her startup to an investor she deeply respects. The investor was warm in the meeting but emailed two days later: 'We're not going to invest at this stage, but please keep us posted.' Priya is on the phone with her cofounder Sam.

Sam: "It's a soft no. Lots of investors say that."
Priya: "She liked it though. I could tell she liked it."
Sam: "She probably did."
Priya: "So why is she not investing."
Sam: "...maybe it's not a no about the company."
